channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla recalling almost 700K vehicles over tire pressure monitors
item.description: Tesla is recalling nearly 700,000 vehicles over an issue with the warning light on the tire pressure monitoring system.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.org, the open source project site that’s the home of the software, plugins, and the WordPress community, but was unclear on when the shutdown would end.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FIFA signs Netflix to US broadcast deal for the Women’s World Cup in 2027 and 2031
item.description: The deal announced Friday is the most significant FIFA has signed with a streaming service for a major tournament. The value was not given, though international competitions in women’s soccer have struggled to draw high-value offers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Asus just launched two business monitors with a unique feature I think all display manufacturers should copy
item.description: Asus just launched two business monitors with a unique feature I think all display manufacturers should copy.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: As Bible sales boom, so does Christian tech
item.description: Want to know the Bible’s stance on technology? There’s an AI chatbot for that. Just a few years ago, experts were predicting a mass decline of Christianity. The percentage of Americans identifying as Christian has fallen almost 20 points since 2000, while the population of non-affiliates was rising.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Automating the entire DevOps toolchain end-to-end
item.description: As enterprise IT environments continue to grow in complexity, organizations are modernizing legacy applications and workloads, amongst other strategic IT initiatives, to address various IT and business challenges—including cost optimization, simplification, time to market, technical debt, scale and
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: VA EHR to Resume Rollout in Mid-2026
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning to resume its rollout of a new Electronic Health Record (EHR) system in mid-2026, after addressing problems at existing sites.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Lenovo’s laptop with rollable screen possibly spotted – and I’m not sure whether it’s a great idea, or a disaster waiting to happen
item.description: Lenovo’s next ThinkBook could embiggen your display for multitasking with a rollable screen – but I’m trying hard not to roll my eyes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon settles worker safety investigation
item.description: Amazon has reached a settlement with the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) over working conditions at the e-commerce giant’s facilities, the Labor Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Windows 11 24H2 strikes a sour note as audio bug hits the update, leaving some PCs silent
item.description: New Windows 11 24H2 bug silences some PCs, and Microsoft has blocked the update for affected devices while it works on a fix.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Los CIO 100 Awards reconocen a Gasib con el Premio a la Innovación 2024
item.description: Una actitud innovadora es clave para las empresas que quieran estar a la vanguardia tecnológica en sus respectivos sectores.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Spending on cloud infrastructure could actually fall in 2025
item.description: Cloud spend is predicted to rise considerably in 2025, but what about the areas predictions don’t cover?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: LA Zoo hatches first-ever perentie lizards, one of largest lizard species in the world
item.description: Two new perentie lizards have hatched at the Los Angeles Zoo, the first of their species to be bred there.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system
item.description: DCSA's new plan for NBIS includes a 36-month product roadmap. The goal is to retire legacy background investigation systems, including PIPs, by fiscal 2027. The post DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How OpenAI’s Jerry Tworek found a new way forward for large language models
item.description: The impressive intelligence gains in OpenAI’s models over time have mainly come from training them with progressively more training data, for longer amounts of time, and with massive computing power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic’s LLMs can’t reason, but think they can — even worse, they ignore guardrails
item.description: Anthropic on Wednesday published a study that explored how its large language model (LLM) deals with conflicting ethical requests. The results show that LLMs can still surprise, something that should scare IT execs preparing to trust generative AI (genAI) results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Holiday Cheer: Arrowhead Gives Away Killzone Crossover Items for Free
item.description: Arrowhead Game Studios gives away Helldivers 2 Killzone crossover items for free following pricing criticism. The developer also plans to bring the items back to the game permanently and extend their availability in the Superstore.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: If Meta prevails against Apple in Europe, AI surveillance will be a feature, not a bug
item.description: Anyone using any digital device needs to wise up to a looming threat as Meta attempts to exploit the Europe Union’s continued assault against the Apple ecosystem to launch what seems to be an open season on your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2024 obituaries: Notable Bay Area residents who died
item.description: SF Giants stars Willie Mays and Orlando Cepeda, tech pioneer Susan Wojcicki and music legend Phil Lesh are among those who left a mark on the Bay Area in their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ireland embraced data centers that the AI boom needs. Now they're consuming too much of its energy
item.description: Dozens of massive data centers are consuming more electricity than all of the urban homes in Ireland and starting to wear out the warm welcome that brought them here.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FAQ: Getting started with Bluesky
item.description: What is Bluesky? Bluesky is a “microblogging” social media network similar to X (formerly Twitter), where people can publish posts with a maximum of 300 characters, as well as follow others, create lists, like posts, and so on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Getting started with Azure Managed Redis
item.description: Microsoft has made a lot of big bets in its preferred cloud-native infrastructure. You only need to look at .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Data on animal movements help Hungarian researchers create a swarm of autonomous drones
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Moving in a dense cloud, like throngs of people walking across a crowded public square, 100 drones maneuver through the night sky in Hungary's capital, the result of over a decade of research and experimentation that scientists believe could change the future of unmanned fli
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: That new Copilot key on your keyboard? It’s useless for business
item.description: Microsoft’s genAI-based Copilot app for Windows doesn’t work with Microsoft’s identity and access management platform Entra, prompting the company to advise organizations to uninstall the app and reconfigure the Copilot keyboard key to open the Microsoft 365 app instead.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: That text from the US Postal Service could be a scam. How to avoid ‘smishing’ fraud
item.description: Be careful in the final week before Christmas, because scammers are trying to use text messages to take advantage of you.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to work with text colors on Linux
item.description: When you list files on the Linux command line, many of the file names show up in colors, as you’ve probably noticed. The colors used depend on the file types. For example, the names of directories will show up in a blue font.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Half a million patients' personal info stolen in massive health care data breach
item.description: Hackers recently leaked personal information of about 500,000 Americans and stole patient medical records that included lab results and insurance details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
